notes: CM 28E scale 0.9984 proposal for seamless national mapping

notes: ZMW numeric 967; 100 ngwee; rebased 1 Jan 2013; 1000 ZMK=1 ZMW

title: Zambia (country profile)
notes: ISO ZM/ZMB/894; TLD .zm; calling +260; date dd/mm/yyyy; corroborate with ISO/IANA/ITU

title: Time in Zambia (IANA Africa/Lusaka)
notes: CAT UTC+02:00; no DST; IANA zone Africa/Lusaka

title: The Republic of Zambia (COMESA country page)
notes: English official + 7 vernaculars; independence 24 Oct 1964; COMESA member

notes: Assented 23 Mar 2021; commenced 1 Apr 2021; establishes Data Protection Commissioner

title: Cyber Security Act No.3 of 2025 & Cyber Crimes Act No.4 of 2025
notes: Repealed Cyber Security and Cyber Crimes Act No.2 of 2021; primary Act text verification pending

title: Constitution of Zambia (Amendment) Act No. 2 of 2016
notes: Art 62(1) National Assembly; Arts 162-163 decentralisation by devolution; cited via 8NDP & Parliament

title: Eighth National Development Plan (8NDP) 2022-2026
notes: Successor to 7NDP (2017-2021); 4th toward Vision 2030

title: The Standards Act No. 4 of 2017
notes: ZABS mandate to develop Zambian National Standards; under Ministry of Commerce Trade & Industry

title: Mining Cadastre (Ministry of Mines) & eGov Portal
notes: Trimble Landfolio; portal.miningcadastre.com; ZIMIS; under Mines and Minerals Development Act

title: National Registration Act & NRC
organization: Ministry of Home Affairs & Internal Security
notes: NRC primary ID issued at 16+; Dept of National Registration Passport & Citizenship

title: Road Traffic Act & vehicle registration plates
notes: LLL NNNN format since 2000; RTSA registration; country code Z

title: Ministries & Cabinet (Cabinet Office)
notes: Ministries directory; corroborated by Cabinet of Zambia list (as of Mar 2025)

title: Engineering Institution of Zambia Act No. 17 of 2010
notes: Statutory professional regulator of engineering; EIZ Act No.17 of 2010 s.3

title: Statistics Act No. 13 of 2018
notes: Establishes ZamStats and national statistical system; primary Act text verification pending

title: Preliminary Annual GDP 2024
notes: Real GDP grew 4.0% (2024) vs 5.4% (2023); current-price GDP K688851m (2024) vs K557406m (2023)

title: Zambia Macro Poverty Outlook / Economic brief
notes: Growth factor-cost 5.7% 2023/2.0% 2024e; int'l poverty $2.15 ~63.7% 2023; inflation 10.9%/15.0%; FDI; fiscal

title: Zambia GDP by sector (World Bank via Statista)
notes: 2023 Agriculture 2.2%; Industry 35.1%; Services 56.8%; mining ~14.3% (2023)

title: 2022 Census of Population & Housing (Preliminary)
notes: Population 19610769 (de facto 8 Sep 2022); male 9603056 female 10007713; households 4056605; density 26.1/km2; avg hh 4.8

title: 2022 Living Conditions Monitoring Survey (LCMS)
notes: Poverty 60% (rural 78.8% urban 31.9%; extreme 48%); LF participation 31.4%; unemployment 13.1%; agri employment 37.2%; hh income rural K2112 urban K5547

title: The Monthly Bulletin Vol 273 Dec 2025 (CPI)
notes: Annual inflation 11.2% Dec 2025; provincial inflation range Luapula 6.8% to Western 15.2%

title: Monetary Policy Report 2026
notes: Inflation 7.1% Mar 2026 (from 11.2% Dec 2025); 6.8% April; target band 6-8%; policy rate 14.25%(context)

title: Zambia Economic brief - labour (World Bank)
notes: Labour force participation ~35%; employment-to-population ~31%; unemployment rose in 2024 among youth/women/rural

title: Zambia economic context / agriculture & drought
notes: Copper main export; only ~15% arable potential cultivated; 2024 drought cereal 1.9Mt (-43%); maize 1.6Mt (~-50%)

title: Zambia installed electricity generation capacity
notes: Installed capacity 3871.32 MW (2024) up from 3811.32 MW (2023); hydro 82% coal 9% solar 5% HFO/diesel

title: National Energy Compact for Zambia
notes: Peak demand ~2500MW; generation fell to ~1019MW Aug 2024 (drought); transmission 12705 km 2023; rural grid connection 5.6%; target +6200MW by 2030

title: Overview of Private Sector Participation in Zambia Energy
notes: National electricity access 53.9% (2023 survey; urban 80% rural 34%); Rural Electrification Act No.5 of 2023; capacity fell to ~1225MW in drought

title: Zambia Core Road Network (RDA)
notes: Total classified road network 67671 km; Core Road Network 40454 km (per RDA; primary doc to confirm)

title: ZICTA 2024 Annual Market Report (reported)
notes: Mobile subs 23.2M (2024) from 21.1M (2023); internet subs 13.5M; towers 3626; sites 14519; mobile-money txn volume +33%; GCI 92.6%

title: Digital 2024 Zambia
notes: Internet users 6.51M (31.2% penetration); mobile connections 16.40M (78.7%); CONTRADICTS ZICTA penetration figure

title: Freedom on the Net 2024 - Zambia
notes: ZICTA Dec 2023: 12.6M internet users (64.1% penetration); fixed-line 99000; 5G launched 2022-2023; 2G/3G phase-out to 4G

title: FinScope Zambia 2020 Survey Report
notes: Financial inclusion 69.4% (6.6M of 9.5M adults); formal 61.3%; mobile money 58.4% (from 14% 2015); excluded 30.6%

title: Zambia Insurance Industry Survey (PwC)
notes: 33 registered insurers; penetration 1.4% of GDP (2020); below African avg ~2.78%

title: VPoA Zambia Progress Report 2024 (transport corridors)
notes: Corridors: North-South Dar-es-Salaam Beira Nacala Walvis-Bay(Ndola-Lubumbashi) Lobito Central; OSBPs Chirundu Nakonde Kasumbalesa Kazungula Mwami; Kazungula Bridge 2021

title: Zambia agro-ecological zones & climate profile
notes: AER I <800mm (12% land); AER II 800-1000mm (IIa/IIb); AER III 1000-1500mm (46% land); growing seasons 80-160 days

title: Drought Management in Zambia 2023/2024 + FAO drought portal
notes: National emergency 29 Feb 2024; 84 of 116 districts; rainfall 35-45% below 1991-2020 mean; Lake Kariba fell to 2.89% capacity

title: FAO drought portal - Zambia river basins
notes: Six major river basins: Tanganyika Chambeshi Luapula Luangwa Kafue Zambezi (within Zambezi & Congo transboundary basins)

title: FISP - Ministry of Agriculture
notes: FISP modalities DIS & e-Voucher via ZIAMIS; redemption: farmer presents NRC+code to accredited agro-dealer, signs GIN, gets SMS; manages contributions/subsidy/insurance

title: ZIAMIS boosts agricultural service delivery
notes: ZIAMIS centralised farmer/agro-dealer registration; 779 agro-dealers accredited (min 7/district); redeem code created within 24h of deposit

title: A smarter way to support farmers in Zambia
notes: e-voucher 220k farmers (17 districts 2023/24) -> ~740k (74 districts 2024/25) -> >1M all districts 2025/26; ~650 registered agro-dealers; mobile-money deposits

title: How FISP e-Voucher is delivering support (PDU)
notes: Farmer contributes K400, govt adds K8000; e-KYC verification reduces ghost beneficiaries; 630+ agro-dealers, 5000+ jobs (2024/25)

title: National Agricultural Extension Services Plan
notes: Block/camp network; extension worker-to-farmer ratio up to 1:1200 (crops), 1:3000 (livestock); Farmer Field Schools & study groups to mitigate

title: MEAS Country Report Zambia 2014
notes: 346 Agricultural Blocks; 1757 Agricultural Camps; Camp Extension Officer is day-to-day farmer contact; ADEO diary; ~76% posts filled (2014)

title: PARM Zambia Feasibility Study WRS 2019
notes: ZAMACE certified WRS; community aggregation centres feed district certified warehouses; quality standards, moisture meters, scales, shellers; FRA alignment

title: FRA maize purchase & smallholder welfare
notes: FRA buys maize at pan-territorial price (often above market) at rural depots; dominant maize buyer; stores/exports/sells to millers

title: ZAMACE commodity exchange & warehouse receipts
notes: ZAMACE online trading platform; warehouse receipts as loan security (Agricultural Credit Act); FRA-ZAMACE partnership for price discovery

title: Advance payments & outgrower schemes (J-PAL)
notes: Outgrower: inputs on credit Oct-Dec; buyback from June with input-debt deduction; village distributors manage 15-20 farmers; Alliance cotton ~35000 outgrowers

title: Commercial Value Chains in Zambian Agriculture (World Bank)
notes: Cotton/tobacco/sugarcane outgrower schemes; distributor scheme (Dunavant) mobilises/contracts farmers, distributes inputs, monitors crops, recovers credit; central contract-farmer registry

title: Agriculture in Zambia (UNCTAD)
notes: Rain season Oct-April; main crops maize/cassava/sorghum/groundnuts/soya; Post-Harvest Survey & Crop Forecast (ZamStats); NRSC (est.1999) remote-sensing crop mapping/yield forecast

title: Zambia Agribusiness Indicators (Gates Open Research)
notes: 400000+ smallholder households in outgrower programs (mainly cotton); agriculture ~70% of labour force; smallholder maize yield ~2.2 t/ha (2011/12)

title: ZIAMIS & FISP e-voucher study (Chikobola & Tembo)
organization: African Journal of Agricultural and Resource Economics
notes: ZIAMIS captures biographical data, farming activities, contact details, linked to NRC; integrates registration/tendering/deposit/payments; accessible national/provincial/district by MoA, agro-dealers, suppliers

title: Digital Technology in Zambian Agriculture (PLAAS WP66)
notes: ZIAMIS provides e-extension via SMS + e-voucher; manages weather index insurance (FISP crops insured); 3.6M farmers registered (2022); Field Area Measure App (AKTC) for GPS field area

title: ZIAMIS universal farmer register (FAO)
notes: Harmonized universal farmer register via ZIAMIS; recommend geodata (farm maps/location) added; routine monitoring every 3 months; ~700k of 1M accessed inputs

title: Agricultural Credits Act No. 35 of 2010
notes: WR must contain: operator name, warehouse location, issue date, serial no, delivery terms, commodity description, storage/handling rate, signature, net weight+grading, negotiable/non-negotiable; depositor discloses particulars/ownership/charges

title: Agricultural Credits and Warehouse Receipts Act 2026
notes: Establishes Electronic Warehouse Receipt System under Warehouse Licensing Authority; integrates Collateral Registry; depositor disclosure of ownership chain & agricultural charge; related: Securities Act 2016, Movable Property (Security Interest) Act No.3 of 2016

title: Digital Registers Transform Farming (agribusinesszambia/FAO)
notes: e-farmer register 4.3M verified/traceable farmers; unique digital ID per farmer/cooperative/vendor; e-Business Directory links farmers to buyers/suppliers/finance/mechanisation; every transaction digitally tracked

title: Zambia mining law overhaul (MRCA 2024) & royalties
notes: Minerals Regulation Commission Act No.14 of 2024 repealed Mines & Minerals Development Act No.11 of 2015; autonomous MRC consolidates Directors of Mines/Safety/Geological Survey/Cadastre; GMDA 2025 (geological survey + ASM); royalties: base 5%, gemstones/precious 6%, copper sliding 4-10%, cobalt/vanadium 8%

title: The Mineral Industry of Zambia 2020-2021 (USGS)
notes: Mining & quarrying ~19.4% of real GDP; ~49% of nontax govt revenue; mining consumes ~47% of electricity; ZCCM-IH state investment vehicle; copper dominates exports

title: Zambia copper mine operations (FQM/Mopani/MMMD)
organization: First Quantum / Mopani / Ministry of Mines
notes: Open-pit (Kansanshi, Sentinel, Lumwana super-pit) & underground (Mopani deep shafts) copper; process crushing/milling/flotation->concentrate->smelting/SX-EW/refining->cathode; ASM formalisation via cooperatives + ASM Fund (safety/health/environment capacity)

title: National Council for Construction Act & contractor grading
organization: National Council for Construction / NCC Act No.13 of 2003
notes: NCC registers/grades contractors (Grades 1-6; categories Building/Civil/Road/Electrical/Mechanical/Specialist A-B); grade sets max tender value; foreign contractors restricted to grades 1,2,A; illegal to operate unregistered

title: Construction Law in Zambia (permits & procurement)
notes: Planning approval from local authority before construction; NCC Act No.13 of 2003 + Local Government Act + building by-laws; completion/occupancy certification; ZEMA approval where required; ZPPA e-GP for public works

title: ZEMA Environmental Management Act & EIA Regulations
notes: EMA No.12 of 2011 (repealed EPPCA; ECZ->ZEMA); EIA process: Project Brief -> ZEMA screening -> ZEMA-registered consultant EIS -> review -> decision letter; monitoring plan, environmental audit 12-36 months, annual reports; EIA Regs SI No.3 of 2026; Central Environmental Information System (s.87)

title: Environmental monitoring
publication: Global Law Experts / NCEA / ITC Labs
column20: WARMA water permits (abstraction/impoundment) via My-WARMA; hydrogeological baseline & monitoring boreholes; NWASCO regulates water/sanitation utilities; air (SO2/NOx/PM/O3) & effluent (BOD/COD/heavy metals/coliforms) monitoring via accredited labs
